diagnosis.final_diagnosis: De Novo microdeletion of BCL11A associated with severe speech sound disorder, childhood apraxia of speech (CAS), and dysarthria
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the proband's heterozygous de novo 0.2 Mb deletion of the BCL11A gene, confirmed through genetic testing. The phenotypic traits included childhood apraxia of speech, dysarthria, generalized hypotonia, and mild intellectual delays, which were consistent with the deletion. The absence of craniofacial or skeletal anomalies, microcephaly, and growth retardation differentiated this case from others with larger deletions in the 2p15p16.1 region.
